segments.name: Goal-Oriented Planners
segments.narrative: Take a disciplined, common sense approach to health and wellness. Set specific goals and create plans to achieve them with fewer barriers to maintaining good health.
bullets: Health & wellness is very important, prioritized most of the time
Put significant effort into their H&W routine with disciplined approach
Pay attention to findings and adjust accordingly
Prioritize diet and exercise over other health behaviors
Look for scientific evidence and set specific goals
Experience fewer barriers to maintaining H&W

segments.name: Practical Minimalists
segments.narrative: Take an 'everything in moderation' approach to health and wellness. Want H&W to be easy and stick to what works with fewer goals and behaviors than average.
bullets: Health & wellness important but take moderated approach
Less trust in experts, more focus on how they look vs. feel
More focused on immediate needs/goals vs. long-term
Approach is spontaneous and journey is personal
Find it difficult to stay motivated
Experience many barriers to maintaining good H&W

segments.name: Impact Seekers
segments.narrative: Health & wellness is essential or very important. More focus on how they look versus how they feel, with more immediate needs and goals rather than long-term planning.
bullets: Like to experiment with new approaches
Share their journey with a community
Seek immediate solutions and experiment with new FN products
Try new products based on hype
Price sensitive but spend more than average on supplements
Go with the flow, live for today, enjoy the moment

segments.name: Set it and Forget it
segments.narrative: Health is holistic and they want H&W to be easy. Take common sense approaches and stick to what works with a personal journey approach.
bullets: Buy out of habit and stick to tried and true
Seek immediate solutions but don't care about sourcing
Price sensitive and spend less than average on supplements
Prefer routine and status quo, avoid risks
Follow others' lead and feel balanced/relaxed
Set small, achievable goals

segments.name: Wellness Optimizers
segments.narrative: Health & wellness is essential to how they live their lives. Focused on a wide variety of health needs and engaged in many H&W behaviors including health tech.
bullets: Put significant effort into H&W routine with disciplined approach
Share journey with community and others seek their advice
Less trust in experts, seek more personalized solutions
Pay more for quality and care about sourcing
Spend more than average on dietary supplements
Express individuality and seek excitement/adventure

segments.name: Conflicted Experimenters
segments.narrative: Health & wellness is very important but they're focused on wide variety of health needs. Experiment with new approaches but find it difficult to stay motivated.
bullets: More focus on how they look vs. how they feel
Experiment with new approaches and find it difficult to stay motivated
Experience many barriers to maintaining good H&W
Actively evaluate options and experiment with new FN products
Seek fortification and values alignment
Stressed/stretched too thin but express individuality

personas.name: Sarah Chen
personas.detail: Sarah meticulously plans her health routine like her marketing campaigns. She researches supplements thoroughly, sets specific wellness goals, and tracks progress. Values scientific evidence and professional recommendations.

personas.name: Mike Rodriguez
personas.detail: Mike keeps wellness simple and practical. He takes basic multivitamins and sticks to what works without overthinking it. Prefers straightforward solutions that fit easily into his busy routine.

personas.name: Ashley Kim
personas.detail: Ashley loves trying the latest wellness trends and sharing her experiences online. She's drawn to products with visible results and enjoys being part of wellness communities. Always looking for the next breakthrough.

personas.name: Tom Johnson
personas.detail: Tom has found a routine that works and sticks with it. He takes the same supplements daily without much thought, preferring consistency over experimentation. Values reliability and simplicity.

personas.name: Dr. Maria Santos
personas.detail: Maria approaches wellness with scientific rigor and personal passion. She uses health tech, tracks biomarkers, and invests in high-quality supplements. Friends often seek her health advice.

personas.name: Jennifer Taylor
personas.detail: Jennifer wants to optimize her health but feels overwhelmed by choices and conflicting information. She tries new supplements frequently but struggles with consistency and motivation.

productStrategy.title: Fast-Acting Functional Nutrition
productStrategy.subtitle: Convenient formats that deliver tangible impact when you need it most

bundles.name: Energy & Focus Bundle
bundles.description: Fast-acting solutions for physical energy, mental clarity, and cognitive performance - ideal for performance-driven consumers

bundles.name: Sleep & Recovery Bundle
bundles.description: Targeted support for sleep quality, stress management, and next-day recovery - addressing immediate wellness needs

bundles.name: Gut Health & Wellness Bundle
bundles.description: Digestive support and overall wellness solutions that provide noticeable benefits and support the gut-brain connection

stat.label: of supplement users look for physical changes as proof products are working

ltvBullets: Target 'in-the-moment' need states where fast-acting benefits provide clear value
Focus on conditions with tangible, noticeable impact like energy, sleep, and gut health
Leverage the blurring lines between supplements, functional foods, and beverages
Prioritize science-backed claims and third-party testing to build trust

marketplace.title: The Evolving Supplement Landscape
marketplace.subtitle: Category blurring creates opportunity for innovative formats

crashing.headline: Functional Nutrition Convergence
crashing.body: The lines between supplements, functional foods, and functional beverages are blurring as consumers use all three to meet similar wellness needs. 73% of supplement users also use alternative products like protein drinks, probiotic foods, and fortified beverages for similar benefits. This convergence creates opportunity for innovative formats that combine the best of all categories.

touchpoints.headline: Multi-Category Competition
touchpoints.ruleOf8: Consumers evaluate 8+ product options across supplements, functional foods, and beverages before choosing
touchpoints.nuance: Choice depends on occasion, convenience, taste preference, and perceived efficacy rather than traditional category boundaries

omniChannel.title: Channel Strategy & Growth
omniChannel.subtitle: E-commerce momentum with strategic retail presence

channels.name: E-commerce
channels.examples: Amazon, DTC websites, subscription models - fastest growing at 10.1%

channels.name: Mass Market
channels.examples: Walmart, Target, CVS - largest channel, steady 5.6% growth

channels.name: Natural/Specialty
channels.examples: GNC, Vitamin Shoppe, health stores - declining share but still important for discovery

channels.name: Practitioner
channels.examples: Doctor offices, clinics - second fastest growing at 7.8%

omniChannel.note: E-commerce projected to become largest channel by 2028, while practitioner channel offers credibility for science-backed products
